title: malus domestica subsp. prunifolia
intro: Malus domestica subsp. prunifolia, also known as the Chinese crabapple, is a subspecies of the apple tree (Malus domestica) native to China and other parts of Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Malus domestica subsp. prunifolia include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed on their own or combined with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
